actions.label: Open Business Bank Account
actions.description: Separate your money. Business dollars and personal dollars stop mixing today.
actions.lesson: Separate business and personal money from day one. Commingling funds pierces your liability protection and turns bookkeeping and taxes into a nightmare.
actions.narrative_success: Two accounts. Two debit cards. It feels like a small thing, but your accountant will thank you at tax time.
actions.narrative_failure: The bank wanted more documentation than expected. You'll get it open next week — minor delay.

actions.label: Set Up Bookkeeping
actions.description: Start tracking every dollar in and out. QuickBooks, a spreadsheet, anything — just start tracking.
actions.lesson: You can't improve what you don't measure. Tracking every dollar is how you spot leaks, prove income to lenders, and decide with facts instead of vibes.
actions.narrative_success: You can now answer 'how much did you make last month' without guessing. That alone is worth the setup.
actions.narrative_failure: QuickBooks is installed. Categories are... a work in progress. But the bank feed is connected.

actions.label: Credit Repair — Dispute Negatives
actions.description: Hire a credit repair vendor to dispute collections, late payments, and inaccurate negative marks on your report. They know the process and fight on your behalf.
actions.lesson: Bad marks aren't permanent. Disputing errors and clearing collections rebuilds the score that decides your access to capital — fix the foundation before you build on it.
actions.narrative_success: The credit repair vendor went to work — three disputes filed, two collections removed, one late payment deleted. Your score jumped twenty points. 'That's just round one,' she said.
actions.narrative_failure: One collection disputed successfully, but the other is fighting back. The vendor says give it another 30 days — the bureaus are slow but she's seen this before.

actions.label: Credit Optimization — Add Tradelines
actions.description: Add authorized user tradelines, report rent and utility payments to credit bureaus, fix data inaccuracies. Build credit depth without disputing anything.
actions.lesson: Your credit score is an asset that prints access to cheap money. A few points decide whether the bank says yes — and at what interest rate. Build it before you need it.
actions.narrative_success: Two tradelines added, rent reporting activated, and a data inaccuracy corrected. Your credit file looks thicker and cleaner. Twelve points up this month.
actions.narrative_failure: The tradeline is pending and rent reporting takes a cycle to show up. Small movement now, but the foundation is set for next month.

actions.label: Aggressive Debt Paydown
actions.description: Throw every spare dollar at your highest-interest debt. It hurts now, frees you later.
actions.lesson: Know the difference: high-interest consumer debt is a wealth-killer — clear it fast. Productive, asset-backed debt is a tool. Eliminate the bad so you can safely use the good.
actions.narrative_success: Two thousand dollars gone from the balance. The interest savings alone will compound from here.
actions.narrative_failure: Paid down most of it. An unexpected expense ate part of the payment. Still progress.

actions.label: Get Income Protection Insurance
actions.description: If you get hurt and can't work, everything stops. This policy keeps the lights on.
actions.lesson: Before you build other assets, your ability to earn IS your biggest asset. Protect the goose before you chase the golden eggs.
actions.narrative_success: Policy in force. Coverage sized to your revenue and liabilities — enough to keep the business running and your family protected for two years if something happens to you. The premium is $150/month.
actions.narrative_failure: Application submitted. Underwriting wants more medical records. Should be approved next month.

actions.label: Debt Restructure with Lending Expert
actions.description: Work with a local lending expert who knows the banks. They move revolving debt into installment loans — the amount depends on your cash and debt position. Dropping utilization is the fastest way to boost your score.
actions.lesson: Moving revolving balances into installment loans drops your utilization — the single fastest lever on your credit score. Same debt, better structure, higher score.
actions.narrative_success: Your lending expert moved revolving debt into a fixed-rate installment loan, dropping your utilization and lifting your score. 'Now you're bankable,' she said.
actions.narrative_failure: One balance moved, but the installment loan terms weren't great. Your expert says wait a month for the score bump to show, then reapply for better rates.

actions.label: Form Basic LLC
actions.description: File a simple LLC to separate yourself from the business legally. It's fast, cheap, and the first layer of protection. You can upgrade the structure later.
actions.lesson: Build the shield before the threat exists. Separating you from the business legally is the cheapest insurance you'll ever buy — and the foundation everything else (business credit, S-Corp, asset protection) is built on.
actions.narrative_success: The LLC is filed, the EIN is issued, and your business exists as its own legal person. Your personal assets just got a wall around them.
actions.narrative_failure: Filed successfully, but the registered agent setup had a hiccup. Resolved, but it cost an extra few weeks.

actions.label: Open Business Line of Credit
actions.description: Get approved for a revolving credit line in the business name. Cash when you need it, interest only when you use it.
actions.lesson: Business credit borrows on the company's name and assets — not yours. This is how you scale with other people's money without putting your household on the line.
actions.narrative_success: Approved for $15,000. The banker shook your hand and said 'come back when you need more.' You will.
actions.narrative_failure: Approved, but only for $5,000. Your revenue history is too short for more. Build the relationship.

actions.label: Set Up Owner Payroll
actions.description: Pay yourself a real salary through the business. Stop pulling random draws — it looks better to lenders and the IRS.
actions.lesson: Pay yourself like a real employee of your own company. It isn't just clean bookkeeping — a documented salary is what unlocks S-Corp tax savings and tells lenders you're a serious, bankable business.
actions.narrative_success: First official paycheck deposited. You're an employee of your own company now. The mortgage lender will like this.
actions.narrative_failure: Payroll is running, but you had to set it lower than planned. Revenue needs to catch up to what you want to pay yourself.

actions.label: Build Banking Relationship
actions.description: Meet with your banker regularly. Move deposits through their bank. Become a known quantity before you need a loan.
actions.lesson: Capital is a relationship business. Bankers fund people they know and trust — build the relationship before you need money, not when you're desperate for it.
actions.narrative_success: The branch manager knows your name now. When you need capital, you won't be a stranger walking in cold.
actions.narrative_failure: Good meeting, but the banker transferred branches. You're starting the relationship over with someone new.

actions.label: Key-Man Insurance
actions.description: Insure the business against losing you — or any critical team member. The business survives if the key person doesn't.
actions.lesson: Insure the people the business can't run without — including you. Key-person coverage turns a catastrophic loss into a survivable one.
actions.narrative_success: Policy in force. If you get hit by a bus tomorrow, the business has a $500K safety net to hire your replacement and survive the transition.
actions.narrative_failure: Application submitted but underwriting flagged a health question. Should clear next month.

actions.label: S-Corp Election
actions.description: Restructure to save on self-employment tax. Your accountant has been telling you to do this for a year.
actions.lesson: How your income is STRUCTURED matters as much as how much you earn. Splitting salary and distributions legally cuts self-employment tax — a lever an employee with a W-2 paycheck will never have access to.
actions.narrative_success: S-Corp election filed. Your tax advisor estimates $8,000-$12,000 in annual savings. Should've done this sooner.
actions.narrative_failure: Election filed successfully, but the accountant's estimate was optimistic. Savings are real but smaller than projected.

actions.label: Premium Financing Strategy
actions.description: Use borrowed money to fund a large whole life policy. Cash value grows tax-free. The bank's money, your wealth.
actions.lesson: The wealthy borrow at low rates to fund tax-free assets. Using the bank's money to build cash value that compounds tax-free is leverage applied to the money engine itself.
actions.narrative_success: The premium financing deal is structured. You're building a tax-free asset with the bank's money. This is how the wealthy stay wealthy.
actions.narrative_failure: Partially funded. The bank's terms were tighter than expected. Still building cash value, just slower.

actions.label: Buy Income-Producing Property
actions.description: Purchase a rental property or small commercial building. Monthly cashflow plus appreciation plus tax benefits.
actions.lesson: This is leverage. You control a six-figure income-producing asset with a fraction of your own cash — the bank funds the rest, the tenant pays it down, and it appreciates while you sleep. Paying all cash would be the INEFFICIENT move.
actions.narrative_success: Keys in hand. The duplex cash-flows $700/month after mortgage, taxes, and insurance. Passive income is real now.
actions.narrative_failure: The property closed, but the inspection found issues. Repairs ate into your projections. It'll cash-flow, just not as much.

actions.label: Build Asset Protection Stack
actions.description: Layer irrevocable trust, umbrella policy, and entity structure so your personal wealth is untouchable. Lawsuits hit the operating company, not you — and the holding company shields accumulated assets from operating risk.
actions.lesson: Wealth you can lose isn't wealth. Trusts and layered entities make your assets untouchable, so one lawsuit can't erase a decade of building. The wealthy protect first, then grow.
actions.narrative_success: The attorney delivered the trust documents. Your assets are now held by entities that don't have your name on the door. Bulletproof.
actions.narrative_failure: The basic structure is in place, but the full trust setup needs more time. You're partially protected.

actions.label: Set Up Holding Company Structure
actions.description: Create a holding company that owns your operating business. Business debts stay with the operating entity — your personal assets and the holding company's assets are isolated. Comes with debt loan provisions, liability shielding, and allows the holding company to manage cash flow, investments, and retained earnings separately from daily operations.
actions.lesson: A holding company owns the assets; the operating company takes the risk. Separating them means a hit to the business can't reach the wealth you've parked safely above it.
actions.narrative_success: Three entities, one holding company, one owner. Each business ring-fenced from the others. Your attorney is impressed.
actions.narrative_failure: The structure is filed. The inter-company agreements need cleanup. Functional but messy.

actions.label: Become a Private Lender
actions.description: Lend your capital to other entrepreneurs' deals. Earn interest like a bank — 8-15% secured by their assets.
actions.lesson: Now YOU'RE the bank. Your capital earns double-digit returns secured by someone else's assets — money working hard while you don't lift a finger. That's passive income.
actions.narrative_success: First deal funded — $20,000 at 12%, secured by the borrower's equipment. Monthly checks arriving. You're the bank now.
actions.narrative_failure: The deal funded, but the borrower's been slow on payments. Secured, so you're protected — but it's stressful.

actions.label: Advanced Tax Strategy
actions.description: Max retirement vehicles, cost segregation on real estate, charitable remainder trusts. Legal, aggressive, effective.
actions.lesson: Taxes are most people's single largest lifetime expense. Legally minimizing them isn't a loophole — it's the difference between building wealth and funding someone else's budget.
actions.narrative_success: Your effective tax rate just dropped from 32% to 21%. Everything is legal. Your CPA high-fived you.
actions.narrative_failure: Some strategies implemented. Others need more documentation. Partial savings this year, full effect next.

actions.label: Apply for Additional Credit Lines
actions.description: With your improved credit profile, apply for additional business credit lines and cards. More available credit means lower utilization and more runway.
actions.lesson: Unused credit lowers your utilization, raises your score, and sits ready as dry powder for the right opportunity. Access is an asset even when idle.
actions.narrative_success: Approved for $15,000 across two new business credit lines. Your available credit just tripled. Utilization dropped to single digits.
actions.narrative_failure: One card approved for $3,000, the other declined. The hard inquiry dinged your score slightly. Your expert says wait 90 days and try again.

actions.label: Secure a Business Term Loan
actions.description: Apply for a fixed-rate business loan through your banking relationship. Use it to consolidate revolving debt, fund growth, or build cash reserves.
actions.lesson: Fixed-rate, predictable debt is a planning tool. Use it to retire expensive revolving balances or fund growth you can confidently pay back — that's productive debt.
actions.narrative_success: Loan approved — $25,000 at 8.5% fixed. The banker smiled when she handed you the docs. Your credit line utilization just dropped to nearly zero, and you have cash to deploy.
actions.narrative_failure: Declined. Revenue history is too short, or DSCR was borderline. Your lending expert says shore up three more months of financials and reapply.

actions.label: Set Up Monthly Tax Reserve
actions.description: Automatically set aside a percentage of revenue each month for taxes. No more year-end surprises.
actions.lesson: Set aside taxes every month and tax season stops being a crisis. That money was never yours — treat it that way and you'll never get blindsided.
actions.narrative_success: Tax reserve is active. Every month, a slice of revenue goes into a separate pot. Future you will be grateful.
actions.narrative_failure: Set up started but your bookkeeping needs cleanup first. Should be running next month.

actions.label: Quarterly Tax Planning Session
actions.description: Sit down with a tax strategist quarterly. Identify deductions, optimize entity structure, project liability.
actions.lesson: Tax strategy is a year-round game, not an April scramble. Planning ahead with a strategist legally keeps more of every dollar you earn.
actions.narrative_success: Your tax strategist found $3,000 in deductions you were missing. She projected your year-end liability — no surprises this time.
actions.narrative_failure: Good meeting, but the strategist needs more documentation. Recommendations next quarter.

actions.label: Open Accumulation Insurance Policy
actions.description: Set up an indexed universal life (IUL) policy that builds tax-advantaged cash value earning ~7% annually. Use it as an emergency fund, tax reserve, or retirement vehicle.
actions.lesson: This is your tax-advantaged money engine. Cash value grows tax-free, you can borrow against it anytime, and at scale it throws off passive, tax-free income. The earlier you start it, the more time compounding has to work for you.
actions.narrative_success: Policy in force. Your first $3,000 is in the cash value. It'll grow at 7% tax-free, and you can borrow against up to 90% of it anytime — no bank approval needed.
actions.narrative_failure: Underwriting needs more info. Should be approved next month.

actions.label: Take a Policy Loan
actions.description: Borrow against your insurance cash value — up to 90%. No credit check, no bank approval, no impact on your credit score. The cash value keeps growing even while borrowed against.
actions.lesson: You just accessed cash tax-free — no bank, no credit check — and your money kept compounding as if you never touched it. This is how the wealthy spend without selling assets or triggering a tax bill. You became your own bank.
actions.narrative_success: Policy loan approved instantly — no credit check, no application. The money is in your account tomorrow, and your cash value keeps compounding as if you never touched it.

actions.label: Get a Bank Loan
actions.description: Walk into your bank and apply for a personal or small business loan. Good credit opens doors — even without an LLC.
actions.lesson: Debt isn't the enemy — UNPRODUCTIVE debt is. Borrowing to grow a business that pays you back is how you buy freedom in years instead of decades. The 'safe' path — a salaried job — trades your time for the most heavily-taxed income there is.
actions.narrative_success: Approved for $10,000. The loan officer barely hesitated — your credit spoke for itself. Cash in account by Friday.
actions.narrative_failure: Declined. The loan officer said your debt-to-income was borderline. Try paying down some debt first.

actions.label: Open 0% Business Credit Card
actions.description: Apply for a business credit card with 0% intro APR for 7-18 months. Use it for cash flow, inventory, or balance transfers. Free money — if you pay it off before the rate kicks in.
actions.lesson: Zero-percent money is a powerful tool when you have a plan to repay before the rate hits — and a trap when you don't. The discipline, not the card, is what makes it work.
actions.narrative_success: Approved for $10,000 at 0% for 15 months. That's free capital for over a year — use it wisely, and pay it off before month 15 when it jumps to 24.99%.
actions.narrative_failure: Approved for a smaller limit — $2,000. The hard inquiry cost you two points. But you're building business credit history now.

actions.label: Balance Transfer to 0% Card
actions.description: Move high-interest revolving balances to your 0% intro rate card. Stops the interest bleeding while you pay it down. Do this before your intro period expires.
actions.lesson: Stop the interest bleed. Moving high-rate balances to 0% buys time to kill the principal instead of feeding the lender — but only with a real payoff plan.
actions.narrative_success: Transferred $5,000 from your 24% card to your 0% card. That's $100/month in interest you just stopped paying. Your utilization spread improved and your score jumped.
actions.narrative_failure: Partial transfer — only moved $2,000. The rest exceeded the available limit. Still, you're saving $40/month in interest.

actions.label: Transfer Personal Debt to Business
actions.description: Use your business credit to pay off personal credit card balances. Cleans up your personal utilization and builds business credit history. Requires an LLC and available business credit.
actions.lesson: Shifting balances onto business credit cleans up your personal utilization (boosting your score) and builds business credit history. Same debt — where it sits matters.
actions.narrative_success: Transferred your personal card balances to the business card. Your personal utilization dropped to near zero — score jumped. The business now carries the debt where it belongs.
actions.narrative_failure: Moved some but not all — the business card limit wasn't high enough for everything. Partial improvement.

actions.label: Fund Insurance Policy
actions.description: Put cash into your accumulation policy — amount scales with your wealth. Grows at ~7% annually, tax-deferred. At $100K+ cash value, you earn passive income through policy loans.
actions.lesson: Keep feeding the engine. Every dollar added compounds tax-free and grows your borrowing base — the bigger the cash value, the larger your future tax-free passive income.
actions.narrative_success: Ten thousand more into the policy. At this rate, you'll hit the passive income threshold within a year. Every dollar in here grows tax-free and can never be taken from you.
